title: Battlefield 6 Season 3 Blast Point | Maps, Weapons, Operators, Battle Pass
subtitle: Season 3 Blast Point arrives June 10 with Cairo Bazaar, Golmud Sands, 6 new weapons, Flashpoint extraction mode, server browser, ranked Conquest, and 2 new operators
thumbnail_alt: Battlefield 6 Season 3 Blast Point, desert warfare with armored vehicles and urban combat
content_html:
Battlefield 6 Season 3, titled Blast Point, launches on June 10, 2026. It is the largest single content update DICE has shipped since the game released in November 2025, delivering two new maps, six new weapons, two new operators, a new vehicle class, a permanent game mode, and three quality-of-life features the community has requested since day one. This is everything confirmed for the update.

Two New Maps | Cairo Bazaar and Golmud Sands

Cairo Bazaar is a full reimagining of Grand Bazaar from Battlefield 3, relocated to modern Cairo. The map keeps the original's tight corridor DNA at a 64-player scale: a central mosque courtyard serves as the highest-value control point, flanked by covered market lanes, rooftop access routes, and a three-story hotel on the eastern edge. Vehicles are restricted to outer ring roads. This is a close-to-mid-range infantry map, and DICE confirmed it launches as a Day 1 option in the Ranked Conquest playlist.

Golmud Sands reimagines Golmud Railway from Battlefield 4 as an open desert armored theater. The collapsed railway runs east to west through the center of the map, flanked by sand dunes, four vehicle depots, and a command bunker on the western end. The new M1128 Mobile Gun System is the signature vehicle for this map. Helicopters and main battle tanks have full access. DICE describes it as "the most pure combined-arms arena" the team has built for Battlefield 6.

Flashpoint | 5v5 Tactical Extraction Mode

Flashpoint is a permanent 5v5 mode with no respawns. One team holds a hard-drive payload at a guarded extraction point. The attacking team must reach the payload, hold it for 15 seconds, and carry it to their designated exit. No vehicles. No spotting UI. Proximity voice chat is on by default in Flashpoint lobbies, regardless of global settings.

DICE is positioning Flashpoint as the competitive entry point for players who find 64v64 Conquest too chaotic. Ranked Flashpoint is planned but not yet scheduled per the April 2026 roadmap. The mode launches across three maps: Hamra Compound, Cairo Bazaar, and Highrise Station.

Six New Weapons | Full Season 3 Arsenal

Season 3 adds six weapons across four weapon classes. All six unlock via the Battle Pass or free challenge track.

Weapon Type Class Access Notable Trait
M60E6LMGSupportBipod deploy cuts recoil by 70% when stationary
AKM-EAssault RifleAll classesUnderbarrel M203 grenade launcher, 2-round mag
TAC-21Marksman RifleRecon, AssaultSemi-auto, hybrid 2x-6x variable scope at base
B&T APC9KSMGAll classes900 RPM, fastest TTK in close range among SMGs this season
M18 ClaymoreGadgetAssault onlyDeployable directional mine, 2 per loadout slot
Type-85 AT RocketGadgetEngineer onlyUnguided high-penetration anti-tank rocket, 3 charges

Two New Operators | Raziya Okafor and Hamid Rostami

Raziya Okafor is a Support operator with a Combat Medic background. Her class specialty is Prone Revive: she can revive downed teammates without standing, cutting the revive exposure window from approximately 2.5 seconds to 0.8 seconds. She also carries a new Field Splint gadget that temporarily halts bleeding damage on multiple nearby squadmates simultaneously.

Hamid Rostami is an Engineer operator built for counter-vehicle and mine-denial play. His class specialty is the IED Sensor, a handheld device that pings the positions of buried mines, tripwires, and concealed vehicles within 25 meters. The sensor emits no audio cue visible to enemies and has a 6-second cooldown. He is the first operator in Battlefield 6 specifically designed to counter Engineer-class gadget spam, which became a dominant meta throughout Seasons 1 and 2. Hamid Rostami is free to unlock by completing the Season 3 Engineer challenge set during the first four weeks.

New Vehicle | M1128 Mobile Gun System

The M1128 Mobile Gun System (MGS) is Battlefield 6's first wheeled fire-support vehicle. It mounts a 105mm cannon with high single-shot damage against vehicles but no explosive splash, making it ineffective against infantry on its own. It reaches 72 km/h on paved road, faster than any armored vehicle currently in the game, and can reverse at full speed. The trade-off is zero armor against RPGs: one direct hit from any Engineer-class rocket destroys it. It spawns exclusively on Golmud Sands at Season 3 launch, with a mid-season update expanding it to Suez Crossing.

Server Browser, Proximity Chat, Ranked Conquest | The QoL Triple

All three features ship June 10. Server Browser opens Community Servers on PC first. Admins can set custom player caps, toggle vehicle access per map, configure round timers, and apply password protection. Console players receive server browser access in the Season 3 mid-season patch, approximately six weeks post-launch.

Proximity Voice Chat activates in a 20-meter radius and includes enemy players in range. It is on by default in Flashpoint lobbies and off by default in Conquest, Squad Rush, and Ranked. Players can disable it globally in settings.

Ranked Conquest is a dedicated 64v64 ranked queue separate from the casual pool. It runs a five-map playlist (Cairo Bazaar, Golmud Sands, Karakoram Ridge, Black Sea Terminal, Hamra Compound) with fixed loadout restrictions: no gadgets above grade 3, no vehicle-mounted grenade launchers. Placements from the earlier Season 2 ranked mode carry over to the new queue.

Season 3 Battle Pass | Desert Spec-Ops, 100 Tiers

The Season 3 Battle Pass runs 100 tiers on a desert spec-ops theme. Key milestone rewards:

  • Tier 1 (free): "Ras Al-Hurr" operator skin in tan and grey desert camo with keffiyeh wrap detail
  • Tier 25: AKM-E "Sand Serpent" gold-and-black weapon skin
  • Tier 50: RPG-7 Gold skin and "Kabul Gold" weapon charm
  • Tier 75: "Mirage Runner" operator skin with heat-haze animated finish
  • Tier 100: "Desert Wraith" full operator set including a gilded AKM-E blueprint, animated skull faceplate with sand-particle effect, and the Blast Point exclusive dog tag

The Battle Pass costs 2,400 BF Coins (approximately $24 USD). A Premium Pass bundle at 3,600 BF Coins includes 30 tier skips at purchase and four additional weapon blueprints not on the standard pass track.

Free Season 3 Rewards

Three items are earnable on the free challenge track, no Battle Pass required:

  • "Wasteland" weapon camo — sand-and-rust finish applicable to all primary weapons. Earned by completing 10 Season 3 Daily Challenges during the first three weeks
  • "Cairo Souvenir" charm — a miniature Cairo Bazaar market stall charm. Earned by getting 25 kills on Cairo Bazaar during launch week, June 10-17
  • Hamid Rostami operator unlock — free by completing the Season 3 Engineer challenge set. Available for the first four weeks of the season

Why This Matters

The server browser is not a cosmetic addition. Community servers have historically extended Battlefield title lifespans by years: BF3 and BF4 maintained active player bases deep into the 2020s largely because of player-run server infrastructure. A stable community server ecosystem turns Battlefield 6 from a rotating live-service into something more permanent, and that changes the calculus for everyone who was waiting to see whether EA would stay committed past the roadmap date.

The Flashpoint mode addresses the second-largest structural complaint since launch, which was the absence of a format that rewarded individual skill at a manageable player count. Whether it becomes the game's ranked backbone depends on DICE following through on the Ranked Flashpoint timeline before the end of 2026. The framework is in place.

For the full Battlefield 6 season history and the Seasons 4 and 5 roadmap, see the Battlefield 6 hub. For the official DICE Season 3-5 roadmap breakdown, see Battlefield 6 2026 Roadmap | Seasons 3-5, Naval Warfare. For more gaming coverage today, see the video games hub.
